text: There are many interpretations of the term illiberalism. Here, I propose to understand illiberalism as a critique of the three main elements of liberal democracy: constitutional governance (the principle of limited state power), the rule of law (which provides the framework for state neutrality), and the openness of liberal societies to a wide range of political actions. Through this lens, the paper outlines the effects and the implications for empirical research on this concept while discussing possible ambiguities in relation to populism and authoritarianism. Lastly, it identifies and describes in depth the main trajectories leading to the phenomenon of illiberalism — authoritarian, traditionalist, religious, libertarian, nativist-nationalist, populist, paternalist, materialist-technocratic and left-wing.
headline: Fully Human Written
